Name: Rat
Combat Mechanic: Low HP, low damage, each rat has a chance to reproduce each turn
Out of combat mechanic: Each rat has a chance to reproduce each dungeon turn

Name: Wailing Banshee
Why do you need to deal with it?: Makes it harder to deal with other monsters
Combat Mechanic: Ranged, Scream drains strength and can cause you to lose a turn
Effective way to deal with it: Attack it with ranged weapons before it drains you
Out of combat mechanic:
Makes a lot of noise
Destroys valuables?

Name: Ghost
Combat Mechanic: Does nothing unless you make loud noises

Name: Ghoul
Why do you need to deal with it?: It will hide, so you’ll lose the chance, and then it will hit you hard
Combat Mechanic: Starts hidden, attacks hard, hides again
Effective way to deal with it:
- Hit it when it becomes visible to attack
- Time the attack, and defend yourself in the turn before, to minimize the impact
-Don't lose sight of it (perception abilities?)
Out of combat mechanic: Finds nearest human, tries to hide or ambush it to feed from it (can attack other monsters)
Movement: Tends to hide? Or moves to ambush if he detects potential victims?
Reacts to light: Moves away so it can hide

Name: Bat
Combat Mechanic: Sees in the dark - so what?

Name: Necromancer
Evokes: His body contains the violent energies he wields
Why do you need to deal with it?: It will keep bringing back enemies, it weakens you
Combat Mechanic: ranged attacks, curses, brings back fallen zombies/skeletons
Effective way to deal with it: kill it fast with ranged attacks? too much like banshee
Out of combat mechanic: summons zombies every 3 turns, always keeps a few around him but if he has enough and he's aware of the player, he sends them to attack him
Movement: Stationary, doing something where he was spawned
Reacts to light:
Carries it
Sends zombies to investigate other lights

Name: Grave Robber
Why do you need to deal with it?: He'll flee and deny you treasure he looted, the bastard
Combat Mechanic: Moves away, then looks for an opening, then flees
Effective way to deal with it: Block its exits and corner him
Out of combat mechanic:
Loots everything it can.
Carries a torch, makes light
Movement: Away from any perceived danger, towards the exit. If reaches the exit, leaves

Name: Zombie
Why do you need to deal with it?: It chases you, though doesn't pose a particular threat
Effective way to deal with it: Destroy its commander, it becomes inert
Out of combat mechanic: Tied and controlled by a necromancer, cultist, or witch
Movement: Intelligently organizes and goes where master commands, back to master if it's under attack

Name: Witch
Combat Mechanic:
Powerful telegraphed magic attacks? 
Puts you in trance/stun?

Name: Skullflower
Effective way to deal with it: Burn it, expose it to light
Out of combat mechanic:
Slowly grows and becomes bigger (HP increases), spreads to other rooms if they're unlit. If lit, it loses HP over time.
Transforms the environment (destroys loot? weakens doors?)?
Movement: No, but it propagates to other rooms
HP:
12 base, increases over time
Fire or light stops growth
Sees in the dark: No vision, but it can sense

Name: Skeleton
Combat Mechanic: Attacks, becomes a pile of bones when defeated, which becomes a skeleton again after a turn
Effective way to deal with it:
Kill it before it comes back
Bludgeoning weapons defeat it without becoming a heap of bones

Name: Gutborn
Why do you need to deal with it?: Will generate larvae, making it harder to eradicate
Combat Mechanic: Whatever the host did + generate larvae
Effective way to deal with it: Kill it while larva, or deal with it as if it was the host (minus advanced abilities)
Damage: Depends on host, can't use advanced abilities (magic, summon, etc)

Name: Gutborn Larva
Evokes: Silent, sneaky, will take you over
Why do you need to deal with it?: Will take over a host and reproduce
Combat Mechanic:
Tries to take you or other creatures over
Telegraphs the attack
Effective way to deal with it: Kill it before it infects anything else, redirect it to enemies with advanced abilities so it takes them over and weakens them
Out of combat mechanic: Infects a corporeal host. After 3 turns, becomes a Gutborn
Movement:
Towards any sign of life, to find a host
Flees the light
Damage: 0, but if it attacks unseen it can take you over
